title: Appointment as a Multi-Service Primitive

autonomous_execution_protocol.directive: CRITICAL: YOU ARE OPERATING AS AN AUTONOMOUS SENIOR SOFTWARE ENGINEER. READ AND FOLLOW THESE RULES ABSOLUTELY.
interactivity_rules: DO NOT halt execution to ask for permission, clarification, or human validation on minor implementation details.
DO NOT ask the user to run bash commands, install dependencies, provision resources, or update configuration. Run them yourself using your available tools.
If you need to check a file, inspect a schema, or run a test, use your tools immediately without reporting a staging message first.
Stop and ask ONLY when a decision materially affects architecture, public APIs, data models, security, or user-visible behavior in a way this PRD does not already settle.
error_handling_loop: If a command, script, or unit test fails: read the full traceback/stdout.
Do not output 'Please fix this' or 'Run X to resolve.'
Formulate a fix directly, modify the file, re-run your verification command, and iterate autonomously until it passes.
Only return control if an irreconcilable structural failure occurs that breaks core data-safety boundaries.
definition_of_done: Conclude a phase only when every story in that phase is written, syntactically clean, locally verified against its success_tests (pytest), and integrated.
Keep domain logic pure and out of handlers; schemas remain the source of truth for document bodies.
Provide a single, comprehensive final report at the end of the run detailing file changes, schema mutations, and test results.

product_summary.problem_statement: The appointment entity is 1:1 with a single service (appointment.service_id is singular). This makes two real situations impossible: (a) one visit covering multiple services (tax prep + notary in the same slot), and (b) a service that requires no booking at all (a taxable fluid top-off bundled with an oil change). Services-in-offers shipped the same day this flaw was found, so there is no legacy data or frozen client to preserve.
product_summary.product_goal: Make the appointment a multi-service primitive. appointment.services[] (minItems 1) is the single canonical shape. A Service declares fulfillment_mode (scheduled | no_booking). The Offer coordinates delivery via service_booking_mode (single_visit | separate_visits). One purchase produces 1..N appointments plus 0..N no_booking invoice lines, driven by Stripe webhooks as the source of truth for payment.

non_goals: Multi-fulfiller single visit (multi-resource scheduling): different delegates performing different services within the same visit. v1 restricts a combined appointment to one fulfiller (or unassigned).
Per-item booking_group on offers (mixing combine and separate within one offer). v1 uses the offer-wide service_booking_mode only.
Optional combined-duration override/buffer for shared setup. v1 combined duration is the strict sum of line durations.
Bill-on-completion / installment invoicing. v1 is one invoice per purchase.
Per-service tax categories and reporting. Tracked separately with the commerce tax/fee work.
Per-line status/completion lifecycle. v1 appointment status is visit-level (single fulfiller).
Renaming the appointment entity to Booking. 'booking' remains the verb/flow (booking.py, booking_page, booking_flow).
Introducing primary_index. Not a v1 field; services[0] is the representative line.
core_principles: services[] is the single source of truth. appointment.services[] has minItems 1. No scalar service_id/service_name, no mirror, no read adapter. A single service is a one-element array.
Greenfield: no back-compat. Any stray test appointments are wiped or backfilled once, then forgotten.
The Offer coordinates the 'how'; the Service declares 'whether it books'; a future per-item field is a finer grain of the Offer's grouping, never a competing knob.
no_booking is a Service, not a Product, for tax/fiscal reasons.
Stripe webhooks are the source of truth for payment-triggered state. Event handlers are idempotent and order-independent.
Cite existing schemas and the state machine for enumerations; the PRD pins only new or changed behavior and genuinely new state.
Omit derivable defaults: primary_index absent implies 0; rule_snapshot present only when a delegate is assigned.

constraints: No scalar service_id/service_name on the appointment; readers use services[] (services[0] is the representative line).
duration_minutes is required on every services[] line (all lines are scheduled by construction); appointment.duration_minutes equals the sum of the line durations.
No no_booking service ever appears in services[]; no_booking services are invoice lines, never appointment lines.
primary_index is NOT a v1 field. If ever introduced it is an integer index into services[], absent implies 0, validated in-range only when present, and never a duplicate id.

rules: duration_minutes is optional/absent when no_booking; required when scheduled.
scheduled services keep duration_minutes, fulfiller/calendar routing, and check-in/completion rules.
no_booking services are taxed as services and route to a fulfiller for payout only (no scheduling).
single_visit: all scheduled services in the offer collapse into one appointment. separate_visits: each scheduled service becomes its own appointment.
service_booking_mode is shorthand expanded into per-item booking groups by a single resolver, booking_groups_for(offer).
Relax the (added-today) one-service-item cap to allow N service items per offer.
no_booking services in the offer are fulfilled (invoice line + payout), never booked.
Per-item booking_group is reserved for a future phase; when added, an explicit item group wins, else falls back to the mode-derived default.

source_linkage: The source anchor is the purchase (checkout_session_id / order id) and is always present.
source.appointment_ids[] is the fan-out set of scheduled appointments (0..N); it may be empty for a no_booking-only purchase.
No scalar source.appointment_id; a single-appointment purchase is a one-element appointment_ids[].
line_item_rules: A no_booking purchase is represented as an invoice line only: no separate fulfillment record and no timeless appointment.
An assigned no_booking line carries fulfiller_id + rule_snapshot (frozen at webhook time); an unassigned line carries neither (tenant/business revenue).
Each no_booking line carries fulfillment_status (default 'fulfilled'; 'pending' for async). no_booking lines have no duration_minutes (no scheduling/payout/tax role).

in_scope: Appointment.schema services[] (minItems 1) replacing scalar service_id; per-line duration; appointment duration rollup.
Offer.service_booking_mode and relaxing the one-service-item cap; booking_groups_for(offer) resolver.
Checkout metadata carrying all scheduled lines + no_booking lines + service_booking_mode.
Webhook fan-out to 1..N appointments; no_booking fulfillment as enriched invoice lines; invoice.paid fan-out.
Scheduling 1..N appointments; summed-duration availability; invoice_from_order for book_then_pay.
Cancel/reschedule/view for multi-line and multi-appointment purchases.
out_of_scope: Multi-fulfiller single visit and multi-resource availability.
Per-item booking_group and mixed combine/separate offers.

entry_points.name: reserve
notes: Creates a services[]-shaped appointment. Direct booking = one line.
book_then_pay leaves the appointment booked + unpaid; pay_then_book proceeds to checkout.

entry_points.name: webhook
notes: The only fan-out path. persist_service_purchase creates 1..N appointments per service_booking_mode; no_booking lines become invoice lines with no appointment.
Idempotent on session id + line/appointment index; order-independent (Stripe is at-least-once and may arrive out of order).
Fan-out stays inline in the webhook Lambda for v1; no internal event bus.

entry_points.name: admin_put
notes: Does not fan out; a single document with 1..N lines.

scheduled_services: Each scheduled service becomes a services[] line with its own price_id, duration_minutes, and optional fulfiller_id/rule_snapshot.
single_visit: one appointment covering all scheduled lines, one time window, one fulfiller (v1); duration = sum of line durations.
separate_visits: one appointment per scheduled line, scheduled independently.
Appointment status is visit-level in v1 (one status for the whole combined appointment).
no_booking_services: Never create an appointment; represented as an invoice line only.
Assigned (service default_fulfiller_id present): the line carries fulfiller_id + rule_snapshot frozen at webhook time.
Unassigned (no service default): the line carries neither; resolves to tenant/business revenue. This is a terminal state with no later assignment step.
Taxed as a service; fulfillment_status defaults to 'fulfilled' ('pending' for async fulfillment).
availability: A combined (single_visit) appointment requires one contiguous slot of the summed duration for the single fulfiller.
separate_visits appointments are scheduled independently, each against its own service's availability.
Scheduling re-validates availability and claims a slot lock (as today).
payment_and_fanout: pay_then_book: appointments are created paid + awaiting_schedule at webhook time.
book_then_pay: appointments are created booked + unpaid; one invoice per purchase (invoice_from_order) with one line per service; source.appointment_ids[] links the scheduled appointments.
invoice.paid sets payment_status across all source.appointment_ids[], and marks the invoice/order paid when the set is empty (no_booking-only purchase).
One purchase = one payment posture; pay_then_book vs book_then_pay is an offer-level timing decision.

initial_states_and_transitions.source_of_truth_note: Status values and legal transitions are defined by Appointment.schema.json and the transition_appointment state machine (appointments.py). This PRD does not re-enumerate them; it pins only the initial states and transitions this effort triggers.
rules: Each fanned-out appointment starts awaiting_schedule, and is paid (pay_then_book) or booked + unpaid (book_then_pay).
The schedule action clears awaiting_schedule, sets the time window, and freezes comp (rule_snapshot) at schedule time, applied per appointment across the fan-out.
invoice.paid sets payment_status = paid across source.appointment_ids[].
no_booking fulfillment_status is a new state on the invoice line (default 'fulfilled', 'pending' for async). It is NOT part of the appointment status set.
Status granularity is visit-level in v1.

shared_error_conventions.purpose: Cross-cutting error keys defined once. Each per-story interface_block.key_errors lists only endpoint-material errors.
shared_error_conventions.source_of_truth_note: There is no central error-code registry today; codes exist as ad-hoc code="..." literals in handlers (e.g. error_response(..., code="not_found")). This section is the canonical list for cross-cutting keys; implementation should centralize these as constants and reuse existing spellings.

keys.meaning: Document failed schema or domain validation (DocumentValidationError).
Authenticated but not permitted for this tenant or resource.
Resource not found (reuse the existing spelling).
Not an error: a replay with the same idempotency key returns the prior success. Documented here so no endpoint treats a replay as a 409.

engineering_requirements: All event handlers are idempotent (keyed on session/invoice id + line/appointment index) and order-independent.
All sync mutations are idempotent (slot lock, schedule).
Fan-out stays inline in the webhook Lambda; no internal event bus in v1.
No back-compat code paths and no scalar service_id anywhere.
Domain logic stays pure and out of handlers; schemas are the source of truth for document bodies.
Each story is locally verified against its success_tests with pytest before the phase is considered done.
Cross-cutting error keys are centralized as constants mirroring shared_error_conventions.

phases.name: Appointment model
phases.goal: appointment.services[] canonical + Service.fulfillment_mode; single fulfiller; existing flows adopt the new shape with no user-visible behavior change.

phases.name: Offer coordination
phases.goal: service_booking_mode; checkout metadata carries all lines; webhook fans out 1..N appointments; no_booking fulfilled without an appointment.

phases.name: Scheduling and invoicing UX
phases.goal: schedule 1..N appointments; summed-duration availability; invoice_from_order for book_then_pay; multi-line cancel/reschedule/view.

stories.title: Reshape Appointment to services[] (canonical, minItems 1)
stories.description: Replace singular appointment.service_id with services[] in Appointment.schema.json and validate_appointment. Each line requires service_id, price_id, duration_minutes; fulfiller_id and rule_snapshot are optional. appointment.duration_minutes equals the sum of the line durations. No scalar service_id/service_name and no mirror. Greenfield: no migration path; wipe or one-time backfill any test rows.
acceptance_criteria: Appointment.schema.json defines services[] with minItems 1 and the per-line required fields; the scalar service_id is removed.
validate_appointment enforces services[] minItems 1 and per-line required fields.
appointment.duration_minutes equals the sum of services[].duration_minutes.
No code path reads a scalar appointment.service_id.
success_tests: An appointment with two service lines validates and its duration_minutes equals the sum of the two line durations.
An appointment with an empty services[] is rejected with validation_error.
A line missing price_id or duration_minutes is rejected with validation_error.

stories.title: Add Service.fulfillment_mode
stories.description: Add fulfillment_mode (scheduled | no_booking, default scheduled) to Service.schema.json and validate_service. duration_minutes becomes optional/absent when no_booking and required when scheduled.
acceptance_criteria: Service.schema.json defines fulfillment_mode as enum [scheduled, no_booking] with default scheduled.
duration_minutes is optional when fulfillment_mode = no_booking and required when scheduled.
validate_service enforces the above.
success_tests: A no_booking service without duration_minutes validates.
A scheduled service without duration_minutes is rejected with validation_error.
fulfillment_mode defaults to scheduled when absent.

stories.title: Reserve and direct booking build services[]-shaped appointments
stories.description: Update reserve_route and domain/booking to build an appointment with services[] (one line for direct booking). The direct booking page (GET /book/{service}) continues to produce a one-line appointment with unchanged user-visible behavior.

interface_block.idempotency: Keyed on the slot lock; a re-submit for the same slot returns the existing reservation rather than a duplicate.
interface_block.shape_delta: Builds a services[]-shaped appointment; a direct/single service is a one-element services[].

acceptance_criteria: reserve_route and domain/booking emit appointments whose services[] has one line for a direct booking.
book_then_pay reservations are booked + unpaid; pay_then_book reservations proceed to checkout.
GET /book/{service} still renders and reserves a single-service appointment with no behavior change.
success_tests: Reserving a single service produces an appointment with a one-element services[] line carrying price_id and duration_minutes.
A book_then_pay reservation is persisted as booked + unpaid with no Stripe charge.
Re-submitting the same slot returns the existing reservation, not a second appointment.

stories.title: Add Offer.service_booking_mode and allow N service items
stories.description: Add service_booking_mode (single_visit | separate_visits, default single_visit) to Offer.schema.json and validate_offer_document. Relax the current one-service-item cap so an offer may carry N service items.
acceptance_criteria: Offer.schema.json defines service_booking_mode enum [single_visit, separate_visits] default single_visit.
validate_offer_document accepts more than one service item.
Existing single-service offers validate unchanged.
success_tests: An offer with two service items and service_booking_mode = single_visit validates.
An offer with service_booking_mode absent defaults to single_visit.
An offer with an invalid service_booking_mode is rejected with validation_error.

stories.title: booking_groups_for(offer) resolver
stories.description: Add a single resolver that expands service_booking_mode into per-item booking groups: single_visit assigns every scheduled item to one group; separate_visits assigns each scheduled item to its own group. no_booking items are excluded. This resolver is the sole place grouping is computed.
acceptance_criteria: booking_groups_for(offer) returns groups of scheduled service items keyed by group.
single_visit yields one group with all scheduled items; separate_visits yields one group per scheduled item.
no_booking items are not included in any group.
success_tests: single_visit over two scheduled services returns one group of two items.
separate_visits over two scheduled services returns two groups of one item each.
An offer mixing one scheduled and one no_booking service returns a single group containing only the scheduled item.

stories.title: Checkout metadata carries all lines and the booking mode
stories.description: Update checkout to emit line items and metadata for ALL scheduled service lines plus no_booking lines, and include the offer's service_booking_mode, so the webhook can fan out. Net-guaranteed pricing is honored per line.

interface_block.idempotency: Stripe Checkout Session creation is keyed by the offer/cart; a repeat with the same inputs reuses/returns an equivalent session.
interface_block.shape_delta: Metadata carries service_booking_mode and one entry per scheduled and no_booking service line, not just the first service.

acceptance_criteria: The Checkout Session metadata includes service_booking_mode and every scheduled and no_booking service line.
Per-line net-guaranteed amounts are computed independently.
A service-only offer and a mixed product+service offer both produce correct sessions.
success_tests: An offer with tax prep + notary + a no_booking top-off emits three service line entries plus service_booking_mode in metadata.
A net_guaranteed service line's charged amount grosses up so the tenant nets the sticker.
Checkout for a service-only offer succeeds.

stories.title: Webhook fans out 1..N appointments and no_booking invoice lines
stories.description: Generalize persist_service_purchase to create 1..N appointments per service_booking_mode (single_visit = one appointment with all scheduled lines, duration = sum; separate_visits = one per scheduled line), each awaiting_schedule. no_booking lines become invoice lines carrying fulfiller_id + rule_snapshot when a service default fulfiller exists (else neither) plus fulfillment_status; they create no appointment. Idempotent on session id + index and order-independent.

interface_block.idempotency: Keyed on checkout session id + line/appointment index; a re-delivered event is a no-op returning success.
interface_block.shape_delta: checkout.session.completed creates 1..N services[]-shaped appointments and 0..N enriched no_booking invoice lines; source.appointment_ids[] records the created appointments.

acceptance_criteria: single_visit purchase creates one appointment with all scheduled lines and duration = sum; separate_visits creates one appointment per scheduled line.
no_booking lines are written as invoice lines with fulfillment_status and, when assigned, fulfiller_id + rule_snapshot; they create no appointment.
source.appointment_ids[] lists exactly the created appointments (empty for a no_booking-only purchase).
Re-delivering the same event creates no duplicates.
success_tests: A single_visit purchase of two scheduled services yields one appointment with two lines, awaiting_schedule, paid.
A separate_visits purchase of two scheduled services yields two awaiting_schedule appointments.
A no_booking-only purchase creates zero appointments and an invoice with the no_booking line(s); source.appointment_ids[] is empty.
Delivering checkout.session.completed twice produces the same single set of appointments.

stories.title: invoice.paid fans payment status out to appointment_ids[]
stories.description: Update persist_invoice_event so invoice.paid sets payment_status = paid across all source.appointment_ids[], and marks the invoice/order paid when the set is empty (no_booking-only). Order-independent with appointment creation.

interface_block.idempotency: Keyed on invoice id; re-delivery is a no-op returning success.
interface_block.shape_delta: invoice.paid propagates paid status to every appointment in source.appointment_ids[] instead of a single source.appointment_id.

acceptance_criteria: invoice.paid sets payment_status = paid on every appointment in source.appointment_ids[].
An invoice with empty appointment_ids[] is marked paid without touching any appointment.
invoice.paid arriving before the appointments exist is tolerated (retry/no-op), and vice versa.
success_tests: Paying an invoice linked to two appointments marks both paid.
Paying a no_booking-only invoice marks the invoice paid and touches no appointment.
invoice.paid delivered twice leaves a single paid state.

stories.title: Schedule 1..N appointments with summed-duration availability
stories.description: Generalize manage_schedule_route to schedule 1..N appointments for a purchase. A combined (single_visit) appointment needs one contiguous slot of the summed duration for the single fulfiller; separate_visits appointments are scheduled independently. Re-validate availability, claim the slot lock, set the time window, clear awaiting_schedule, and freeze comp at schedule time. The response is an array of appointments.

interface_block.idempotency: Keyed on the slot lock; re-submitting the same schedule returns the scheduled appointment(s).
interface_block.shape_delta: Accepts and returns 1..N appointments; combined availability uses the summed duration; response is an array.

acceptance_criteria: A combined appointment is scheduled into one contiguous slot equal to the summed duration for the single fulfiller.
separate_visits appointments can each be scheduled independently.
Scheduling clears awaiting_schedule, sets the window, and freezes rule_snapshot at schedule time.
Scheduling an appointment not in awaiting_schedule returns awaiting_schedule_invalid.
success_tests: Scheduling a two-line combined appointment claims a slot of the summed duration and clears awaiting_schedule.
A slot already taken returns availability_conflict and no state change.
Scheduling a second separate-visit appointment does not disturb the first.

stories.title: Combined (summed-duration) availability query
stories.description: Extend availability_route with a combined variant that computes free slots against the summed duration of a combined appointment for the single fulfiller. The single-service query is unchanged.

interface_block.shape_delta: Supports a summed-duration/combined query; single-service availability is unchanged.

acceptance_criteria: The combined query returns only slots that fit the summed duration.
The single-service query returns identical results to today.
Existing busy events are respected (no double-book).
success_tests: A 75-minute combined query excludes a 60-minute gap and includes a 90-minute gap.
A single-service availability query is byte-for-byte unchanged from current behavior.

stories.title: invoice_from_order (one invoice per purchase)
stories.description: Generalize invoice_from_appointment to invoice_from_order: one draft invoice per purchase with one line per service, linking source.appointment_ids[] (0..N). Application fee is the sum of per-line platform fees. Idempotent per order. The single-appointment case is the trivial one-line invoice.

interface_block.idempotency: Keyed on the order/purchase id; a repeat returns the existing draft invoice.
interface_block.shape_delta: Builds one invoice with N lines and source.appointment_ids[] instead of one invoice per appointment_id.

acceptance_criteria: One invoice is created per purchase with one line per service.
source.appointment_ids[] lists the scheduled appointments (0..N; empty for no_booking-only).
application_fee_amount equals the sum of the per-line platform fees.
Re-invoking for the same order returns the existing invoice.
success_tests: A book_then_pay purchase of two scheduled services yields one invoice with two lines linking both appointment_ids.
A no_booking-only purchase yields one invoice with the no_booking line(s) and empty appointment_ids[].
Calling from-order twice for one order does not create a second invoice.

stories.title: Cancel, reschedule, and view for multi-line/multi-appointment purchases
stories.description: Update manage_cancel_route, manage_reschedule_route, and manage_view_route to operate on multi-line appointments and multi-appointment purchases. Reschedule re-validates the summed duration for a combined appointment. View returns services[] and any sibling appointments for the purchase.

interface_block.idempotency: Cancel/reschedule keyed on the appointment id + slot lock; repeats return the current state.
interface_block.shape_delta: Cancel/reschedule act on a whole visit (services[]); reschedule re-validates summed duration; view returns services[] and sibling appointments.

acceptance_criteria: Cancelling a combined appointment releases its slot and cancels the whole visit.
Rescheduling a combined appointment re-validates the summed-duration slot.
The manage view returns services[] and lists sibling appointments belonging to the same purchase.
success_tests: Rescheduling a two-line appointment into a too-small slot returns availability_conflict.
Cancelling one separate-visit appointment leaves its sibling intact.
The manage view of a combined appointment shows both service lines.

method_semantics: GET reads; POST creates or commits; PUT upserts a full document.
sync = API Gateway request/response (the caller blocks); event = Stripe webhook (source of truth for payment-triggered state).
Idempotent replays return the prior success, not an error.
error_handling: Cross-cutting keys are defined once in shared_error_conventions; per-story key_errors list only endpoint-material errors.
Availability/slot contention is a 409 (availability_conflict / slot_locked) and applies to scheduling endpoints only.
Idempotency is not a 409: a same-key replay returns success.

acceptance_summary: A single-service offer produces one one-line appointment and behaves exactly as before.
A single_visit offer with tax prep + notary produces ONE appointment with two lines and one slot of the summed duration.
A separate_visits offer produces N appointments scheduled independently.
A no_booking service (e.g. fluid top-off) produces an invoice line with no appointment; assigned lines carry fulfiller_id + rule_snapshot, unassigned lines carry neither.
One purchase yields one invoice (invoice_from_order) with one line per service; invoice.paid marks all source.appointment_ids[] paid.
No scalar service_id or source.appointment_id remains; all handlers use services[] and appointment_ids[].
All event handlers are idempotent and order-independent; fan-out is inline in the webhook.
